summary: End-to-end validation of the new client review workflow (tokenized invites → public /review/:token → admin approval → homepage replacement). Backend: 23/23 new pytest pass covering POST /api/admin/review-tokens (auth 401 no/wrong token, 60-day expires_at, send_email=true does not 500), GET /api/reviews/invite/{token} (404 unknown, 200 fresh with {token,client_name,project}, 410 after use), POST /api/reviews (marks token used; 2nd use → 410; unknown token → 404; photo_data_url not starting with data:image/ → 400; rating=0 and rating=6 → 422; quote<10 chars → 422), GET /api/reviews public (only approved, token field stripped, sorted by approved_at desc), GET /api/admin/reviews (auth required, returns reviews+tokens with url ending in /review/{token}), PATCH /api/admin/reviews/{id} (auth required, approved_at set/cleared, 404 unknown id, controls public visibility), DELETE /api/admin/reviews/{id} and DELETE /api/admin/review-tokens/{token} (both auth + 404 unknown), contact regression. Frontend verified via Playwright on preview URL: /review/INVALID_TOKEN_xyz renders error card 'This link isn't valid.' with 'Go to mo home' link; /review/{valid} pre-fills Name=client_name, renders 'Hope UI_Project is going well.' intro, 1-5 star picker, short quote (<10 chars) triggers 'Quote should be at least 10 characters.' toast and blocks submit, valid submission shows Thank-you screen (data-testid=review-success); Homepage Testimonials with zero approved reviews renders hardcoded 3 (A. Rivera/Daniel F./Priya R.) across mobile+desktop carousels; after approving 1 review the hardcoded set is FULLY replaced (only UI_TestClient shown, no Rivera/Daniel/Priya mixing); /admin ReviewsPanel renders above Leads inbox with data-testid='reviews-panel', invite-form creates token (with send_email=false path tested — no 500), active-tokens list displays client name + url + Copy button, Publish/Unpublish moves review between Pending and Live sections in-UI, toast 'Published to site.' confirms. Test data cleaned up after run.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py line 292 token generation: `secrets.token_urlsafe(6).replace('-','').replace('_','')[:10].lower()` — after stripping URL-safe chars and slicing to 10, the effective entropy can drop to ~6-8 chars of [a-z0-9]. 60-day window is fine, but collision loop only iterates while a hit is found. Consider token_urlsafe(16) truncated to 16 chars, or just use hex(12). Minor.
server.py PATCH /api/admin/reviews/{id} accepts a raw dict body — replace with a Pydantic model (e.g. ReviewApproval) for input validation parity with the rest of the API.

server.py /api/reviews submit_review: on token-already-used the token stays used but DB inserts a second review if somehow retried before update_one lands — the check+update is not atomic. Low probability, but consider using $set with filter {token, used_at: None} and raising 410 if matched_count==0.

server.py list_approved_reviews: photo_data_url for every approved review is shipped to every homepage visitor (can be ~50-400KB each × 100 reviews = 40MB). When scaling past ~20 reviews, consider serving a lighter list and lazy-loading photos.
ReviewPage.js uses `a href='/'` instead of react-router `<Link>` — forces a full reload on the error/thank-you state. Cosmetic but violates SPA routing consistency (rest of app uses Link).

Admin.js ReviewRow: Publish/Unpublish/Delete buttons lack data-testid (only the container panels do). Adding data-testid='review-publish-{id}', 'review-unpublish-{id}', 'review-delete-{id}' would harden future regression tests. Tests currently fall back to text selectors.

ReviewSubmit.photo_data_url max_length=500_000 chars but frontend enforces 350KB post-base64 — fine, but backend should also count bytes of actual data-url content after the 'data:image/...;base64,' prefix to avoid sneaking binary via prefix obfuscation.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (error state, pre-fill, star picker, short-quote toast, submit, thank-you, hardcoded fallback, approved replacement, admin panel create+publish+unpublish)

seed_data_creation: Pytest module creates ~10 tokens and ~3 reviews prefixed TEST_ under test_reviews.py — cleaned in module teardown. Frontend test created 1 review token (UI_TestClient) + 1 invite (UI_AdminTest) and deleted both at end.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reviews workflow fully green. Key data-testids: review-name, review-role, review-company, review-quote, review-rating, review-star-{1..5}, review-photo-upload, review-photo-preview, review-photo-remove, review-submit, review-success, reviews-panel, reviews-refresh, invite-form, invite-name, invite-email, invite-project, invite-submit, invite-send-email, active-tokens, pending-reviews, published-reviews, testimonial-{0..2}, testimonial-desktop-{0..2}. Note: /api/reviews returns FULL photo_data_url inline — monitor payload size when >20 reviews approved. Publish/Unpublish buttons on individual review rows lack data-testids; next iteration could add 'review-publish-{id}' etc.
rca of the issue: N/A — no defects found. Workflow meets every acceptance criterion in the brief.
